differential_diagnoses: Seizure disorder secondary to cerebral injury
next_steps: Aggressive seizure management and antiepileptic drug initiation
Lumbar puncture to rule out meningitis or subarachnoid hemorrhage
Investigation of underlying triggers (e.g., pain medication-induced or inflammatory state from sickle cell crisis)
Continuation of supportive care including fluids and pain management

differential_diagnoses: Seizure disorder secondary to acute cerebral injury
Cerebral microhemorrhage secondary to chronic sickle cell disease
next_steps: Maintain strict blood pressure management to target safe ranges for PRES
Initiate continuous EEG monitoring to assess for subclinical status epilepticus
Consult neurology for management of acute symptomatic seizures
Consult hematology for optimization of sickle cell management and assessment of transfusion therapy
Review all current medications for potential neurotoxic or seizure-threshold lowering effects
Obtain follow-up head imaging to assess for resolution or evolution of vasogenic edema
Evaluate renal function and electrolytes to rule out metabolic contributors to encephalopathy
